failed_rules.message: Delayed Financing is not an allowed loan purpose for 2nd Lien - FAIL
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
Occupancy 'Investment Property' not eligible. Available: Primary Residence
Super Jumbo program only available for Primary (Owner Occupied) occupancy - Non-Owner Occupied not eligible
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
DSCR Multi (5-8 Units): Only Residential 5-8 Units and Mixed Use 2-8 Units are eligible
Delayed Financing is not an allowed loan purpose for Investor DSCR No Ratio - FAIL

specifications: Must be a tri-merge or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) from acceptable agencies (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include all borrowers on the loan
Must verify Social Security number for all borrowers
Must show credit scores from all three bureaus
Must include complete tradeline details with payment history
Must show mortgage/housing payment history if available
Must include inquiries, public records, disputes, collections, charge-offs, and alerts
Must certify results of public record searches for each city where borrower resided in last 2 years
Must be dated within 120 days of closing
reasons_needed: Credit score of 800 must be validated to confirm program eligibility for Investor DSCR
Tradeline and credit history requirements must be reviewed per NQMF guidelines
Credit report is needed to verify no adverse credit events, disputes, or derogatory items
Housing payment history on existing investment property mortgage must be reviewed
Required for all borrowers per NQMF underwriting guidelines

specifications: Must be a valid, unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must show borrower's full legal name matching the loan application
Must include photograph, date of birth, and expiration date
Acceptable forms: U.S. Driver's License, U.S. Passport, State ID Card, Military ID

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity must be verified before any loan documents can be validated
Photo ID required to confirm borrower identity matches across all loan file documents

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower signature and date
Must include correct borrower name, SSN, and current address
Must specify tax years for transcript request (most recent 2-4 years as applicable)
Must be signed within 120 days of closing

reasons_needed.reason: NQMF Guidelines require a fully executed and signed IRS Form-4506C for all documentation types
Tax transcript authorization required for income verification and fraud prevention

specifications: Must certify subject property will be held as investment/non-owner-occupied
Must confirm borrower will not occupy the property as primary or secondary residence
Must confirm property will not be occupied by any family member or LLC member per DSCR guidelines
Must include borrower signature and date
Must document borrower's current primary residence address
Must provide proof of ownership (mortgage statement, deed) OR active lease agreement if renting
For rent-free arrangements, must provide rent-free letter from property owner
Primary residence should be in different geographic location from subject property OR demonstrate superior general appeal/location compared to subject

reasons_needed.reason: DSCR program requires property cannot be occupied by borrower(s), any member of the borrower's LLC, or any family member
Occupancy status drives LTV limits, reserve requirements, and documentation rules
DSCR guidelines require borrowers who own a Primary Residence must provide proof of ownership or evidence they are living in a property owned or rented by their spouse/domestic partner
Primary residence documentation required to confirm borrower will not occupy investment property

document_requests.document_type: Borrower Certification as to Business Purpose
specifications: Must identify borrower Aubrey Banaga
Must identify subject property address
Must affirm business/investment purpose for refinance
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Must confirm property is not and will not be owner-occupied
Must be consistent with investment property occupancy declaration on 1003
Must state the loan proceeds are for business/investment purposes
reasons_needed: DSCR Investor program is a business-purpose loan product requiring affirmation of non-owner-occupied investment intent
Business purpose affidavit confirms loan is exempt from consumer lending regulations (TRID/ATR)
Required to document that the refinance is for investment property and business purposes consistent with DSCR program requirements
Occupancy and business purpose representations must be consistent and documented for compliance
document_requests.cure_path: Borrower must sign and date Business Purpose Affidavit affirming investment/business purpose

specifications: Must cover 12-month lookback period for existing mortgage on subject property
Must identify borrower name and property address
Must show monthly payment amounts and due dates
Must show payment status for each month (on-time, late, delinquent)
Must identify any late payments with number of days late (30/60/90+)
Must be from current mortgage servicer or credit report tradeline
Must support 0x30 payment history requirement for rate/term refinance
reasons_needed: Housing history verification is required for all programs per NQMF guidelines
Payment history on existing mortgage affects refinance eligibility
Late payments on current mortgage may affect qualification or pricing adjustments
DSCR refinance programs typically require clean mortgage payment history
Verifies borrower's track record of meeting housing obligations

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 1007 for single-family or Form 1025 for 2-4 units
Must identify the subject property address
Must include appraiser's opinion of market rent
Must reflect terms of current lease if property is tenant-occupied
Must be completed by the appraiser as part of the appraisal engagement
Must support DSCR ratio calculation using gross rental income
reasons_needed: DSCR ratio calculation requires market rent documentation via Form 1007/1025
Guidelines require FNMA 1007/1025 for all DSCR refinance transactions
Monthly gross rents are used to determine DSCR = Gross Rental Income / PITIA
document_requests.guideline_reference: LONG TERM RENTALS – DSCR SUPREME AND INVESTOR DSCR: FNMA 1007/1025 required for refinance

specifications: Must be fully executed lease agreement for the subject property
Must have no less than 1 month remaining at time of closing
Must identify tenant name and lease terms
Must show monthly rent amount
Must identify property address matching the subject property
If month-to-month tenancy, must provide signed extension letter or bank statements to support rent deposits
reasons_needed: All DSCR refinance transactions require the property to be leased per guidelines
Lease agreement confirms tenant occupancy status and rental income amount
Guidelines state vacant property on refinance is not eligible for DSCR loans
1007/1025 must reflect terms of current lease if property is tenant-occupied
document_requests.guideline_reference: DSCR RATIO - All refinance transactions require the property to be leased. Vacant property is not eligible.

specifications: Must provide rent loss insurance coverage for the subject property
Must equal at least 6 months of PITIA
Blanket policies covering the subject property are acceptable
Must identify the subject property address
Must show coverage amount and policy effective dates
Must be hazard insurance policy covering fire and standard extended coverage perils
Extended coverage must include: wind, civil commotion, smoke, hail, aircraft, vehicle, explosion
Must provide replacement cost coverage (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must name all persons holding title as named insured
Must include standard mortgagee clause (loss payable clause not acceptable)
Must include NQM Funding, LLC as mortgagee with correct address
Must show property address matching subject property
Must show effective date and policy must be effective for at least 60 days after funding
Must show coverage amount, deductible, and premium
Must require insurer to notify mortgagee before cancellation
Policy number and insurance company name must be clearly indicated
reasons_needed: Rent Loss Insurance is required for DSCR loans per NQMF guidelines
Coverage must equal at least 6 months of PITIA to protect rental income stream
Required to ensure continued debt service coverage in case of tenant vacancy
Hazard insurance is required to protect collateral against loss or damage
Investment property requires adequate property insurance coverage
Mortgagee clause protects lender's interest in the collateral
document_requests.guideline_reference: RENT LOSS INSURANCE (DSCR): Rent Loss Insurance for the subject property is required and must equal at least 6 months of PITIA

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 months (60 days)
Must include all pages even if blank
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or last 4 digits acceptable)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each statement period
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must cover all accounts being used for funds to close and reserves
Large deposits (typically 25% or more of monthly qualifying income or inconsistent with account history) must be identified for potential sourcing
Must show sufficient verified balance to cover required months of PITIA reserves
Must identify acceptable asset source (checking, savings, money market accounts)
Must show account ownership matching borrower name
Must be dated within 90 days of note date
Investment property DSCR loans typically require 6+ months PITIA reserves
If reserves held in multiple accounts, all accounts must be documented
Balance must be liquid and accessible (not subject to withdrawal penalties affecting availability)
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close including closing costs and prepaid items per NQMF guidelines
Reserve requirements must be supported by verified liquid assets
Account ownership must be confirmed to match borrower on loan application
Transaction history required to identify any large deposits requiring additional documentation
NQMF requires assets dated within 90 days of note date
DSCR investment property loans require reserves based on program requirements and risk factors
Reserve calculation must be supported by verified assets showing sufficient liquidity
Reserves provide evidence of borrower's ability to manage property expenses if rental income is interrupted
Investment property loans have heightened reserve requirements compared to owner-occupied

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 1004 for 1-unit single family residence
Must identify subject property address matching 123 Main St, San Diego, CA
Must identify property type as single-family residence and unit count as 1
Must show appraised value supporting the $625,000 value and 65% LTV calculation
Must be dated within 365 days of Note date; recertification required if over 120 days
Must include all pages, addenda, and appraiser certification
Must include street map showing location of subject and all comparables
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan
Must include original color photographs of front, street, rear views, kitchen, bathrooms, main living area
Must include comparable sales analysis and sales comparison approach
Must confirm property condition is C4 or better (C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must confirm property meets minimum standards: average or better condition, no health/safety violations
Must confirm property is not rural (rural properties not permitted)
Must confirm minimum 400 square feet with full kitchen and bathroom
Must disclose any repairs, adverse conditions, or inspection requirements
Must include appraiser's state license/certification number
Must include copy of appraiser's E&O insurance policy
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation is required for underwriting all mortgage transactions
LTV/CLTV eligibility depends on appraised value confirmation
Property type and unit count affect program eligibility for Investor DSCR
DSCR properties are expected to be free of deferred maintenance per guidelines
Property condition C4 requires evaluation for marketability and acceptance on DSCR loans
Subject property identity must be consistent across the loan file
document_requests.source_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Appraisal Requirements 1-4 Unit Residential

specifications: Must be submitted through UCDP (Uniform Collateral Data Portal)
SSR score must be 2.5 or less for acceptable appraisal review
Must reference the subject property appraisal report
Must identify any flags, warnings, or data quality issues
If SSR score exceeds 2.5, alternative review product required (AVM, desk review, or field review)
reasons_needed: Appraisal review process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
FNMA SSR score of 2.5 or less satisfies the appraisal review requirement
Required to validate appraisal quality and data consistency

specifications: Must be FNMA Form 3170 (Assignment of Rents)
Must identify subject investment property address
Must be executed by all borrowers on title
Must assign rental income/rents to the lender as additional security
reasons_needed: Required for all investment properties per NQMF appraisal requirements
Provides lender with assignment of rents as additional collateral security
Subject property is an investment/non-owner-occupied property
document_requests.source_reference: NQMF Guidelines - Appraisal Requirements 1-4 Unit Residential

specifications: Must be a FEMA Flood Certificate (Life of Loan flood determination)
Must identify subject property address
Must indicate flood zone designation (Zone A, V, or non-SFHA)
Must indicate if property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA)
Must indicate if property is in Coastal Barrier System (CBRS) or Otherwise Protected Area (OPA)
Must be provided by FEMA-approved flood determination vendor
reasons_needed: Determination of flood zone is required to establish flood insurance requirements
Properties in Zone A or V require flood insurance and must be in FEMA participating community
Appraisal flood zone must reconcile with flood certificate determination

specifications: Must identify borrower Aubrey Banaga as owner/vested party
Must identify subject property address and legal description matching appraisal
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, exceptions, and title requirements
Must show title insurer duly authorized in California
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQMF guidelines
Must be dated within acceptable recency window
Effective date must be within 90 days of closing
Must confirm first lien position can be achieved after payoff of existing mortgage
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership and insurable interest per NQMF Title Insurance requirements
Chain of title review required to verify vesting history and identify any flipping activity
All exceptions must be reviewed against NQMF allowable title exceptions list
Existing liens must be cleared or paid at closing for refinance transactions
First lien position must be confirmed without exception
document_requests.trigger: All loans require title commitment review

specifications: Must show current vesting in borrower Aubrey Banaga's name
Must include complete legal description of subject property
Must be recorded deed (Grant Deed, Warranty Deed, or equivalent)
Must reconcile with title commitment vesting information
Recording information must be legible and complete
reasons_needed: NQMF requires final title policy vesting to reflect borrower name
Vesting must align with loan application and title commitment
Property ownership must be confirmed for collateral security
Deed type affects property rights and title insurance coverage

specifications: Must identify current lender/servicer of existing mortgage being refinanced
Must include loan/account number
Must show total payoff amount including principal, interest, and fees
Must include good-through date (valid at least through anticipated closing)
Must include per diem interest amount for payoff calculation
Must identify subject property address securing the existing lien
Must include wire instructions or payoff remittance address
reasons_needed: Refinance transaction requires payoff of existing first lien mortgage
Payoff amount required to calculate loan proceeds and verify lien position
NQMF guidelines require existing liens affecting title to be paid at closing
Title company must disburse payoff directly to existing lender at closing
Good-through date ensures accurate funds calculation for closing
document_requests.trigger: Refinance transaction - existing mortgage must be paid off
related_guidelines: JUDGMENTS, LIENS AND IRS TAX REPAYMENT PLANS
TEXAS HOME EQUITY - PAYOFF OF DEBT
